action_choice_hints: Whether the action cell is part of a contiguous segment of target tokens in its row or column
If the action cell is the first target token encountered from a boundary along a row or column
Whether the action cell is adjacent (orthogonally) to an empty cell
If all cells between the action cell and a boundary in a given direction are target tokens
Whether the action cell is within a contiguous run of target tokens of length at least K
If the action cell is the only target token in its row or column
Whether the action cell is at the edge of a contiguous block of target tokens
If the action cell is in the topmost or bottommost row containing any target token
Whether the action cell is the nearest target token to a boundary in its column
If the action cell is part of a vertical or horizontal band of target tokens
Whether the action cell is surrounded on all four sides by target tokens
If the action cell is the last remaining target token in the grid
Whether the action cell is at a corner of a contiguous region of target tokens
If the action cell is the only target token adjacent to a particular empty region
Whether the action cell is part of a group of target tokens separated from others by empty cells
transition_hints: Selecting a target token cell causes that cell to become empty in the next state
Only the chosen action cell changes state; all other cells remain unchanged
The removal of a target token does not affect the state of adjacent cells
No new target tokens are created as a result of an action
Empty cells remain empty after any action
The set of target tokens strictly decreases by one with each action
The action is only valid on cells currently containing a target token
The environment does not introduce obstacles or new regions after an action
The grid structure and boundaries remain fixed throughout transitions

diversity_requirements: Include templates that capture both local (adjacency) and global (row/column/region) patterns
Ensure templates cover both edge/corner and interior cell scenarios
placeholder_usage_limits: Limit the number of direction placeholders per template to at most 2
Restrict the maximum run-length parameter K to 4
avoidances: Avoid templates that only check for the presence of a target token at (s, a)
Avoid templates that depend solely on absolute coordinates
Avoid templates that duplicate coverage of identical spatial relations
